title: Complete Pipeline Failure - All Video Types Not Generating
description: After upgrading to Claude Sonnet 4.5, a new job resulted in complete video generation failure across all renderer types (WAN, Manim, others). Analytics show incorrect counts. The issue requires systematic isolation of the failure point in the pipeline.
symptoms: All Manim sections failed to render
Insufficient logging to diagnose root cause

recent_changes: Upgraded Manim code generator from Claude 3.5 Sonnet to Claude 4.5 Sonnet
Updated system prompt with strict 80-line limit and anti-truncation measures
Added code stats logging (lines, chars, token usage)
context.environment: Docker container with Python 3.12, Manim v0.19.0, full LaTeX stack

hypothesis: Possibility 2: Prompt format incompatible with Claude 4.5
Possibility 3: Code generation producing invalid Python
Possibility 4: Manim rendering encountering new errors

investigation_approach.strategy: Systematic isolation via enhanced logging and component testing
phases: Phase 1: Add debug logging to all pipeline components
Phase 2: Create isolated unit tests for each component
Phase 3: Run tests to identify exact failure point
Phase 4: Fix identified issue and verify

manim_code_generator: Log section title and narration segment count before generation
Log Claude API response status and body
Log extracted code length and preview
Log code stats (lines, chars, token usage, finish reason)
Add warnings for long code or high token usage
renderer_executor: Log output file path and size
wan_video_generator: Log section ID and prompt preview
Log API endpoint and request payload
flask_app: Log input data preview for each phase

isolated_tests.test_manim_spec: Verify Gemini 2.5 Pro generates valid manim_scene_spec
isolated_tests.test_manim_code: Verify Claude 4.5 generates valid Python code
isolated_tests.test_manim_render: Verify Manim v0.19.0 can render test code
isolated_tests.test_wan_api: Verify WAN video API is accessible

action_items.item: Get job ID and inspect presentation.json
Run isolated tests to identify failure
